title: Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi
tagline: Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi is a much-loved spiritual town in Gujarat, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: Shitala Mata Temple is a c. 12th century Hindu temple dedicated to goddess Shitala located in Butapaldi village of Mehsana district, Gujarat, India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Bhandu
topPlaces.description: Bhandu is a village in Visnagar Taluka of Mahesana district in Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Bokarvada
topPlaces.description: Bokarvada is a village in Visnagar Taluka of Mahesana district in Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Dudhsagar Dairy plant
topPlaces.description: The Dudhsagar Dairy plant is a milk processing plant located in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. Owned and operated by Dudhsagar Dairy, a cooperative, it is a postmodern brutalist building designed by Achyut Kanvinde.

topPlaces.name: Biladi Baug
topPlaces.description: Biladi Baug, officially known as Mahatma Gandhi Garden, is an urban park in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was established in 1946 and is being redeveloped in 2025 by the Mahesana Municipal Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Rajmahal, Mehsana
topPlaces.description: Rajmahal is a palace in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. Built in 1904 by Sayajirao Gaekwad III of Baroda State, it was used as the government office and later as the court until 2017.

topPlaces.name: Sardar Patel Stadium, Mehsana
topPlaces.description: Sardar Patel Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium and sports complex at Municipal Ground, Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was built at the total cost of ₹11 crore (US$1.2 million) and opened in October 2022.

topPlaces.name: Para Lake
topPlaces.description: Para Lake, officially Swami Vivekanand Lake, is a man-made lake located in Mehsana city in the Indian state of Gujarat. Excavated during the Gaekwad rule, it was redeveloped and opened in 2019.

topPlaces.name: Jetalvasana
topPlaces.description: Jetalvasana (Jetalvāsaṇā) is a village of Visnagar Taluka in Mehsana district in the Indian state of Gujarat with a population in 2011 of 3311 persons.

items.note: Bhandu is a village in Visnagar Taluka of Mahesana district in Gujarat, India.
Bokarvada is a village in Visnagar Taluka of Mahesana district in Gujarat, India.

The Dudhsagar Dairy plant is a milk processing plant located in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. Owned and operated by Dudhsagar Dairy, a cooperative, it is a postmodern brutalist building designed by Achyut Kanvinde.
Biladi Baug, officially known as Mahatma Gandhi Garden, is an urban park in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was established in 1946 and is being redeveloped in 2025 by the Mahesana Municipal Corporation.
Rajmahal is a palace in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. Built in 1904 by Sayajirao Gaekwad III of Baroda State, it was used as the government office and later as the court until 2017.

Sardar Patel Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium and sports complex at Municipal Ground, Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was built at the total cost of ₹11 crore (US$1.2 million) and opened in October 2022.
Para Lake, officially Swami Vivekanand Lake, is a man-made lake located in Mehsana city in the Indian state of Gujarat. Excavated during the Gaekwad rule, it was redeveloped and opened in 2019.
Jetalvasana (Jetalvāsaṇā) is a village of Visnagar Taluka in Mehsana district in the Indian state of Gujarat with a population in 2011 of 3311 persons.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (72 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (76 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi?
faq.a: Stays in Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sitala Mata Temple, Butapaldi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bhandu, Bokarvada, Dudhsagar Dairy plant, Biladi Baug, Rajmahal, Mehsana.
